full_response:
Let  

S₁ , S₂ , … , S₁₀  = the ten individual sums obtained when the two fair dice are
rolled.

Event  
E = “every one of the ten rolls produces neither a 5 nor a value ≥ 8”.

The random variable of interest is  

X = S₁+S₂+⋯+S₁₀  (the total of the ten sums) conditioned on E.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
1.  Distribution of a single roll under the conditioning
--------------------------------------------------------------------
For one fair roll the possible sums and their multiplicities (out of 36 equally–likely
outcomes) are

2 (1), 3 (2), 4 (3), 5 (4), 6 (5), 7 (6), 8 (5), 9 (4), 10 (3), 11 (2), 12 (1).

Under event E we may only keep the values  
2, 3, 4, 6, 7.

Total favourable outcomes: 1+2+3+5+6 = 17.

Hence, for k∈{2,3,4,6,7}

  P(S = k | E) = (# of outcomes for k)/17.

Summarising:

P(S=2)=1/17, P(S=3)=2/17, P(S=4)=3/17, P(S=6)=5/17, P(S=7)=6/17.

Because the original rolls are independent, the rolls remain
independent after conditioning; thus S₁,…,S₁₀ are i.i.d. with this
conditional distribution.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
2.  Mean and variance of one conditioned roll
--------------------------------------------------------------------
Let μ = E[S | E] and σ² = Var[S | E].

Compute the first moment:

μ = (2·1 + 3·2 + 4·3 + 6·5 + 7·6)/17
   = (2 + 6 + 12 + 30 + 42)/17
   = 92/17
   ≈ 5.4117647.

Second moment:

E[S² | E] = (2²·1 + 3²·2 + 4²·3 + 6²·5 + 7²·6)/17
           = (4 + 18 + 48 + 180 + 294)/17
           = 544/17
           = 32.

Therefore

σ² = E[S²] − μ²
    = 32 − (92/17)²
    = 32 − 8464/289
    = 9248/289 − 8464/289
    = 784/289
    = (28/17)².

--------------------------------------------------------------------
3.  Variance of the sum of ten independent rolls
--------------------------------------------------------------------
With independence,

Var[X | E] = Var[S₁+⋯+S₁₀ | E]
           = Σ_{i=1}^{10} Var[S_i | E]
           = 10·σ²
           = 10·(784/289)
           = 7840/289
           ≈ 27.1280.

--------------------------------------------------------------------
\boxed{\displaystyle \operatorname{Var}(X\,|\,E)=\frac{7840}{289}\approx 27.13} 

Confidence: 0.91
